questions.question: Two answer choices both technically work. How do you usually pick the best one?

answers.text: Match the answer to the question's priority word (cost, availability, performance, security, or least overhead)
Always choose the cheapest option regardless of the requirement

questions.feedback: The priority word tells you which Well-Architected pillar the question optimises for, and that breaks the tie.

questions.question: You're spending too long on a hard question. What's the best move?

answers.text: Flag it, make your best guess, and move on; return later
Leave it blank to save time
Spend as long as needed even if it costs you later questions

questions.feedback: Flag and move on so you don't starve later questions. Never leave blanks, there's no penalty for guessing.

questions.question: Which are good exam-preparation practices?

answers.text: Take full-length timed practice exams and review every answer
Get hands-on in a real AWS account using the Free Tier
Prioritise the security domain, since it's weighted highest
Memorise answers to a leaked question dump instead of understanding concepts

questions.feedback: Timed practice, hands-on labs, and prioritising the heaviest domain all help. Relying on dumps doesn't build the reasoning the scenarios require.
